EDI Analytics Engineer Exercise

Learner progression signals by competency

A governed DuckDB model and director-facing Dash dashboard.

V

10-minute walkthrough: data quality, model design, indicator logic, dashboard, and next steps.

Vanderbilt University School of MedicineGitHub repository01 / 09
The ask

Turn raw educational data into a governed progression indicator and dashboard.

Source files
4

Learners, sessions, crosswalks, and assessment events.

Assessment events
1,751

Mixed scores, dates, IDs, pass/fail records, and outliers.

Review audience
2

Program director and curriculum committee reviewing progression signals.

Problem framing02 / 09
Data Quality

Preserve raw values. Normalize only what is defensible. Flag the rest.

Normalization

Trim, uppercase, de-hyphenate, and zero-pad IDs for joins; for example, vu-12 becomes VU0012.

Ambiguous records

Keep duplicate/session variants and suspicious score values visible; for example, S005 title/date conflicts and a 999/25 score stay flagged.

Metric exclusions

Exclude pass/fail, missing max score, out-of-range score, orphan learner, and unmapped session events from the numeric indicator.

Privacy

Use learner IDs in the dashboard and omit direct names/emails from dashboard.

Source data handling03 / 09
Model design

A small dimensional model keeps the dashboard explainable.

Raw CSVs

Loaded as-is for auditability.

dim_learner

Learner ID, cohort, status.

dim_session

Session ID, date, title.

dim_competency

PCRS competency labels.

→

fact_assessment_event

One row per assessment event with quality flags.

→

progression mart

One learner + competency.

Fact grain

One row per assessment event, preserving all source events even when excluded from the indicator.

Mart grain

One active learner + competency, ready for cohort-relative dashboard reporting.

Why this helps

Dashboard numbers can be traced back to the source event and the exact eligibility rule.

DuckDB + SQL layers04 / 09
Indicator method

The signal compares a learner’s competency average to the right peer benchmark.

01

Valid events

Passed quality checks, matched learner/session records, mapped to a competency.

02

Average

Calculate each learner’s average score within each competency.

03

Evidence

Require at least two valid scored events before interpreting status.

04

Benchmark

Use the cohort + competency median of learner averages.

05

Status

Review suggested, on track, strength, or limited evidence.

Cohort-relative competency progression indicator05 / 09
Status language

The dashboard separates performance signal from evidence coverage.

Strength

At least two valid events and 10+ percentage points above cohort/competency median.

On track

At least two valid events and within ±10 percentage points of the benchmark.

Review suggested

At least two valid events and 10+ percentage points below the benchmark.

Limited evidence

Exactly one valid event, so the dashboard does not overinterpret the signal.

Transparent status rules06 / 09
Director-facing output

The dashboard turns the model into a review workflow.

Live: edi-analytics-eng-demo.onrender.com07 / 09
SQL implementation

The indicator logic is visible in the mart, not buried in the chart.

-- Final dashboard-ready status rule
case
  when valid_event_count = 0
    then 'not_scored'
  when valid_event_count = 1
    then 'limited_evidence'
  when benchmark_learner_count < 3
    then 'limited_benchmark'
  when learner_avg_score_pct
       - cohort_median_score_pct <= -0.10
    then 'monitor'
  when learner_avg_score_pct
       - cohort_median_score_pct >= 0.10
    then 'strength'
  else 'on_track'
end as monitor_status

Readable rule

The dashboard labels map directly to the SQL case statement.

Evidence-aware

Low evidence and weak benchmarks become statuses, not hidden caveats.

Easy to revise

The 10 percentage-point threshold can be changed after stakeholder review.

SQL files on GitHub08 / 09
If this moved beyond the exercise

Potential next steps.

Validate semantics

Confirm cohort meaning, competency mapping, and threshold usefulness with EDI/faculty stakeholders.

Represent pass/fail evidence

Decide how professionalism attestations should sit alongside numeric score progression.

Add automated checks

Tests for orphan learners, missing crosswalks, out-of-range scores, and duplicate session conflicts.

Deploy securely

Move to the team’s analytics stack, potentially Azure App Service, with scheduled rebuilds and managed access.

Questions?09 / 09
